prose:
Method provenance & key results
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, f⁰ and dual descriptor for ferrocene; identification of the most electrophilic (largest f⁺) and most nucleophilic (largest f⁻) sites.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) as implemented in the xtb 6 program.
Geometry provenance: single-point Fukui analysis performed directly on the user-supplied geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz); no further optimization (GFN2-xTB//user geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cationic state (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anionic state (charge –1, multiplicity 2) used for the finite-difference Fukui derivatives.
Software: xtb 6 (called via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill).
Convergence & caveats: run completed; integrity check status "ok" (trustworthy = true).  The following warnings were issued by the engine and are reproduced verbatim:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Live log file: written during the run by chemkit as <skill>_<timestamp>.out in the current working directory (available for inspection with `tail -f`).

Headline numerical findings (GFN2-xTB, gas phase)
Most electrophilic atom (largest f⁺): Fe (atom 11)  f⁺ = 0.22885456
Most nucleophilic atom (largest f⁻):  Fe (atom 11)  f⁻ = 0.25938191

Abridged Fukui indices (per atom, 1-based indexing):
Atom  f⁺        f⁻        f⁰        Dual
 1 C  0.0136383 0.0121792 0.0129088  0.0014592
 … (symmetry-equivalent cyclopentadienyl C/H atoms omitted for brevity) …
11 Fe 0.22885456 0.25938191 0.24411824 −0.030527354

The full table, dual descriptor map, and the plot image are available in the output directory along with the JSON result file (`…/live.json`).
